entry.title: vacancy report

Good Day,

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas Camera IPad Auto focus
entry.content:
Hi, 

Please try the following approach:

  1. When attachment dialog is opened, there are two options: Start Camera and Browse Files
  2. Select Browse files
  3. Select Take Photo or Video
  4. Native camera app will have focus control
  5. Take photo
  6. Select Use Photo
  7. Select created photo in Slipstream Engine and push Confirm

So you have to use Browse Files and then use native camera to access the focus.



Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Watching out for Bugs

I will let you know

entry.title: How to update LIPS-EXBWR field with external value for batch splitted item while IBD creation.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have requirements to do MIGO with  external value in movement type 503 and not on MAP rate. Plant & storage location is EWM managed.

There is a field MSEG-EXBWR enhanced on MIGO screen. On saving on MIGO screen IBD number is generated.

W.r t IBD there is a process of doing GR in EWM through tcode /n/SCWM/PRDI. GR is done, Warehouse task is created WH Order is created and confirmed to get the GRN number. The GR is posted on MAP value and not the external value entered on MIGO screen.

How to update LIPS-EXBWR on saving of IBD, for the batch splitted line item?

Best Regards,

Narendra

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas Camera IPad Auto focus

entry.title: Re: MDG Web Dynpro Create Material- Page Header Already Exists
entry.content: Do you have found any resolution on this. Thanks

entry.title: Streamlined Dangerous Goods Regulatory Updates
entry.content:
Two Ways to Receive Updates

Public Cloud customers can now receive new and updated dangerous goods compliance requirement versions (CRV) through two convenient methods:

  1. Mime Repository: For those not using our Content Service, CRV updates can be accessed directly from the Mime Repository.
  2. Regulatory Content Service: CRV updates can be loaded from the Content Service directly into your database tables, making them immediately available in your SAP system.

 Activation Still Required

This user-dependent approach offers you the flexibility to choose the method that best suits. However, using the Activate Compliance Requirements – Dangerous Goods app, you still need to review and activate the new CRV. Otherwise, the new version will be in the system, but not in use.

 Improved Efficiency

Previously, content was packaged with normal S/4HANA system updates approximately 10 times per year, with no alternative options available. This new content delivery method streamlines the process, ensuring you have access to the latest dangerous goods compliance information as soon as it becomes available.

 Future Developments

While this new content delivery method is currently available for public cloud customers, we are actively working on extending this functionality to our private cloud and on-premise customers. Stay tuned for updates found in our Roadmap, as we continue to enhance our services to better serve your needs.

 We are excited to bring you this improved content delivery system and look forward to helping you navigate the complex world of dangerous goods compliance with greater ease and efficiency.

For information on how to set-up the Regulatory Content Service for Public Cloud, see this SAP Note.

For information on how to set-up the Regulatory Content Service for Private Cloud and On-Premise, see this SAP Note.

entry.title: How can I use drag and drop in "Schedule Sequence manually" Heuristic in PPDS?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In another company, when we were using "Schedule Sequence manually" Heuristic in PPDS we were able to drag and drop one or multiple orders with the mouse. So we first used some sorting options and then manipulated the result in the spreadsheet manually with the mouse, before we executed the new schedule to the planning board. 

Now in a new company, it seems this is not standard functionality, but was enabled somehow. Does anybody know if there is an OSS note or something that can do this?

Thanks in advance!

Stocki

entry.title: Re: How can I use drag and drop in "Schedule Sequence man...

entry.title: SAP Build version not the same as tutorials.
entry.content: Hi everyone. I am a complete newbie just dipping my toe into the SAP Build world. I am trying to add an API to my app and have watched and read countless tutorials on how to do it. The API i am using works as i have tested it on "POSTMAN" with no issues. The problem i have is that my interface does not correspond to the one in the tutorials. I click the "Data" tab, all good. I click on "Create Data Entity" but, the option i get is "Universal REST api Integration", and not" REST Api Integration" as shown in all the tutorials i have seen/read. The input fields it provides do not correspond to the tutorials either and only allow a base URL. I am on the community edition. when i first started using it, all the fields complemented the training, but it then suddenly changed and now only have the "universal "option. Have i inadvertently changed something or does the community addition have restricted functionality? any help/advice or guidance would be gratefully received to help me move forward. Thanks very much in advance

entry.title: Re: How to get URL parameters in SAP Screen Personas
entry.content:
For me similar URL works. So just check what happens with "window.location", then "window.location.search" and so on.

Maybe you are using not supported browser.

There is an example:

entry.title: Re: BTP Trial RAP - GUI access
entry.content:
@Shky Please use the Feedback button and then contribute in the tutorial itself. 

This will create an issue that will go to the tutorial author directly. Otherwise the tutorial author might not see this thread.

entry.title: Where can I get the skills to use monitor and performance tools within SAP HANA Cockpit?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Using Monitoring and Performance Tools in SAP HANA learning journey

Overview
This SAP Learning Journey equips you with skills to use monitor and performance tools, troubleshoot performance issues, investigate SQL statements, address hardware performance issues, manage workload, and use capture and replay using SAP HANA cockpit. You will gain practical knowledge through hands-on exercises and real-world scenarios. By the end, you'll be proficient in investigating system performance within your organization. Get ready to embark on this exciting and comprehensive journey!

Learning objectives

  • Develop proficiency in using monitor and performance tools within SAP HANA Cockpit for effective system performance analysis.
  • Acquire the skills to troubleshoot performance issues, including investigating SQL statements and addressing hardware performance issues.
  • Gain expertise in workload management to ensure efficient system operation and resource utilization.
  • Learn how to utilize capture and replay functionalities to simulate and analyze system workloads, enabling informed decisions for performance improvements.

Prerequisites

  • Installing and Administering SAP HANA

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content: @mwn - Consider using the Feedback button and then choosing contribute in the tutorial itself.  That's the best way to communicate this directly with the tutorial author.

entry.title: Re: Error CSRF Token Validation Failed in POST API
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Iam able to get the csrf token using the get request and iam passing it to the post request to insert data but it is giving the error as 403 csrf token validation failed.

but the same above process is working in the post-man.

and in some documentation some mentioned to use the cookie in the post request which we got from the get request but for me this cookie is not getting exposed in my response header.

iam using the approuter to solve the CORS issue

Here is mta yml file

 

modules:
- name: testing-approuter
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: testing-approuter
  properties:
     
      CORS:
        - uriPattern: "/"
          allowedMethods:
            - GET
            - POST
            - PUT
            - PATCH
            - DELETE
          allowedOrigin:
            - host: '*'
          maxAge: 3600
          allowedHeaders:
            - Authorization
            - Content-Type
            - X-CSRF-Token
            - Accept
            - set-cookie
            - Cookie

          exposeHeaders:
            - X-CSRF-Token
            - vary
            - set-cookie
          allowedCredentials : true
     
  parameters:
    keep-existing-routes: true
    disk-quota: 256M
    memory: 256M
  requires:
  - name: test-destination
  - name: test-connectivity
resources:
- name: test-destination
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: destination
    service-plan: lite
- name: test-connectivity
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: connectivity
    service-plan: lite

entry.title: Mass changes to field " Person Responsible" in Cost centers in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi ,

Could anybody advise if there is any app available in SAP Public cloud to perform mass changes to Cost Centers for field "Person Responsible"

While there are many apps available for Mass changes, I am unable to find any app specifically for Cost center mass changes

Your valuable inputs will be of great help!

Thanks in advance

Regards

entry.title: Re: How to open simulation test mode in a new browser tab?
entry.content: It depends on where you are opening it from. If the object you are linking it from, and the simulation itself are hierarchically below the same structure that was originally opened (i.e. in the same Book) then it will open in a virtual tab. If you want to force it to open in a new browser window, select the Open in New Window option for the link, and that should do it. (But if you are trying to do this from the playback button for the simulation mode, then no not possible without building your own launch page.)

entry.title: Re: PurRqs will be generated when Available Qty -1000 always
entry.content: One last idea - is there any discontinuation information in MRP 4. If there is - that could be another rootcause. If not, I will not be able to help.

entry.title: Re: How to create a curriculum with item A and following item B with retraining?
entry.content: I would think you would want to develop 2 curriculums for this. First for users who haven't completed Item A, then for those that 'have' completed Item A. The assignment profile includes an Item matches or Item Doesnt match option. -- At least that is probably be how I would do it.

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Three
entry.content:
no Daniel this is not what i need 

Let me explain more...

let's imagine now i have a dash board showing some data (amount spent by some customers )

  • measure: money
  • dimension: customer name

now i have 2 input controls: year and Month

Month filter has all the values Jan - Aug in September 2024 (cause until Aug the data were loaded)

my need is always when I'm loading the dashboard or open every time Month input control should be checked with the prior month like before the completed posting month in this case it should checked by default July.

 

and it should be dynamically happen for every month. 

is it possible by Scripting using any [ID]'s

entry.title: Re: SSR Challenges in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content: @JanWidmer Doc is right, full page caching in SSR is not recommended. Native Composable Storefront SSR doesn't have cache for subroutine. Yes, I'm talking OCC response + processing as BaseSites computation. This can be setup in cache to optimise SSR response time for all rendering pages.

entry.title: Navigation to URL in RAP
entry.content:
Hi Community,

 

We would like to have a navigation to an external URL in RAP list report. First option is obvious and it works properly: Use a column in list with annotation: type: #WITH_URL

But we would like to add some extra possibilities: Use some code to create several url's and launch them with only one click. In old ECC systems, we have cl_gui_frontend_services=>execute that easily solve this issue and we thought it would be great to use something similar inside a RAP Action. Unfortunately, that class is not supported in Public Cloud.

 

Is there any workaround to perform this navigation via code?

 

Thanks a lot,

Kr,

Agustín.

Agustín.

entry.title: Re: Need to find BOM chang history CDS View for changes d...
entry.content:
Hello @pdattatray 

 

For BOM Change Documents we have the  app 'Display Change Documents' ( App Id CA_SHOW_CD ) allows the viewing of the change documents from CDHDR/CDPOS

 If you run it with Change Document Object : STUE it will show you the change documents for BOM's

 

An email notification for BOM changes is not available in S/4HANA Cloud . There is the functionality of "Change Records" you can use instead

 

Best Regards

Enda

As I don't see a separate ROMANIA Prod Tax portal authentication URL's, can you please suggest the steps to generate the Access token for Production connectivity?

Thanks in advance,

Veera

entry.title: Material Inspection in Warehouse Advance Return Management
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

We are using advance return management in ECC to inspect the materials for return inbound deliveries via

MSR_INSPWH tcode.

It works fine if we have all same materials and same batch in same HU.

e.g Mat XYZ and Batch XYZ in same HU.

But if return delivery have multiple HU's and same material batches are in other HU it gives error

 

Please let me know if anyone of you have came across similar issue ?

Thank you in advance.

regards,

Ricky

entry.title: Time Calculation Data Action - From To Period
entry.content:
Hello

I need to make a data action that deletes data in a specific period using the date parameters  [p/LockedFrom] and [p/LockedTo] on the site dimension [d/Site].

Any ideas how to achieve this?

entry.title: Messages between Iflows from different Runtimes
entry.content:
Hi there,

is there a way to send/receive messages from one runtime to another with an "internal" method.

I would like to get incoming requests from the cloud integration runtime. Ideally i could address the message via jms or ProcessDirect to route it to the runtime of the edge integration cell. I don't want to use an extra instance of an cloud connector for that matter. This would cost extra ressources and extra messages would be applied. 

Are there any solutions?

Kind regards,

Florian

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Remote Table dynamic filter pushdown
entry.content:
Hi @RG20 

I have tried with below SQL script view in Datasphere pointing to a Snowflake view.

DECLARE myArticleString NVARCHAR(5000) = '000000000010347199';

return select "MATERIAL" from "V_ORDER_FORECAST" where "MATERIAL" in (myArticleString);

This worked more or less as expected generating the following query on the Snowflake DB:

SELECT "V_ORDER_FORECAST$VT"."MATERIAL" FROM "FORECASTING"."V_ORDER_FORECAST" "V_ORDER_FORECAST$VT" WHERE ("V_ORDER_FORECAST$VT"."MATERIAL" = '000000000010347199')

So the "In" is converted to "=" which does not matter for a single article. 

However, I have not yet succeeded in pushing a dynamic in-string list of multiple articles, (which could be populated dynamically based on eg an excel upload). I have tried variations over below SQL script view.

I would like to avoid having to hardcode lots of articles into the view but instead reading them from another table. If someone should succeed with that, I would love to hear about it, thanks 🙂

DECLARE myArticleString NVARCHAR(50000);

myArticleString = '''000000000086640951'',''000000000010519311''';

return select "MATERIAL" from "V_ORDER_FORECAST" where "PLANT" = '1399' AND "RUNDATE" = '20240821' AND "MATERIAL" in (:myArticleString);

Br.

Michael

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Clean Core Extensibility with ABAP Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Thanks, Mani and Vignesh for this insightful session. Helped reinforce clean core principles as well as RAP fundamentals. Keep up the good work!

Couple of misses that could have added more value to the session:
1. Processes of C1 releasing a wrapper

2. ATC checks and governance process

3. Demo of the wrapper generator you talked about initially

Additionally, reiterating what others have requested:
1. Access to the code repo

2. Presentation

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: How to search within this managed tag (SAP SQL Anywhere)?
entry.content: I guess this is not possible then?

entry.title: Re: SAP EAM - Epic, Feature Example
entry.content:
Here is an example from Copilot of how you might structure an epic, feature, and user story in the context of SAP EAM:

Epic:

  • Title: Improve Maintenance Management
  • Description: As a company, we want to enhance our maintenance management processes to reduce downtime and improve efficiency across all our facilities.

Feature:

  • Title: Implement Preventative Maintenance
  • Description: As part of improving maintenance management, we need to implement a preventative maintenance system that schedules regular maintenance tasks to prevent equipment failures.

User Story:

  • Title: Schedule Regular Maintenance Tasks
  • Description: As a maintenance manager, I want to schedule regular maintenance tasks for all critical equipment so that we can prevent unexpected breakdowns and ensure smooth operations.
  • Acceptance Criteria:
    • The system should allow the maintenance manager to create and schedule maintenance tasks.
    • The system should send reminders to the maintenance team before the scheduled maintenance tasks.
    • The system should log all completed maintenance tasks for future reference.

This structure helps in breaking down large initiatives (epics) into smaller, manageable pieces (features and user stories) that can be worked on incrementally.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard and Universal Model OData Integration with S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
There are several strategies on how to read and manipulate data from external application in SAP PaPM Cloud. However there are instances wherein if database are not exposed, we are only limited to do specific pulling mechanism. In this blogpost, I will share with you step by step guide on how you can leverage S/4HANA Cloud APIs to be used in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model (SM) and Universal Model (UM).

 

Prerequisite

  1. You have access with proper admin roles to an S/4HANA Public Cloud system
  2. You have destination administration access to SAP BTP where your SAP PaPM Cloud resides. Including SAP PaPM Cloud roles for Modeling and Connection configuration.  

 

Scenario:

User1 is tasked to read general ledger account line items from S/4HANA Public Cloud System. User1 saw in the help portal documentation that there is an API OData V2 which can be used for it which is G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X). User2 on the other hand wants to read consolidation task and assignments of consolidation document type and found an API Odata V4 which can be used: Consolidation Task ‒ Read

Both users would want to use SAP PaPM Cloud SM or UM to check if they can read on through using above API Service.

Read on through for the steps 😊

S/4HANA Cloud Setup

There are three main setup to be done here namely: Maintaining Communication User, Creation of Communication System and Creation of communication arrangements, read on below to see how it can be done:

1.      Create Communication User

  1. In your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system, go to Communication Management .
  2. Choose Maintain Communication Users Tile
  3. Choose New and in the dialog box which appears enter your desired UserName, Description and Propose a password. Please take note to save the password into a secured location on your local since it will not appear again once you hit Create.
  4. Choose Create

2.      Create Communication System

  1. Go back to Communication Management and choose Communication Systems Tile
  2. Choose New and in the dialog box which appears enter your desired System ID. System Name will automatically be populated. Choose Create
  3. Since we are only aiming for inbound connection to PaPM Cloud, choose the checkbox for Inbound Only.
  4. Choose Users for Inbound Communication tab and Choose ‘+’ button.
  5. Add the Communication User created in Step 1 then choose OK.
  6. Choose Save.

 

3.      Create Communication Arrangement

  1. Go back to Communication Management and choose Communication Arrangements Tile
  2. Choose New and in the dialog box which appears enter the Scenario and desired Arrangement Name.
    Note: You can verify what Communication Scenario you need in the API Resource Attribute section in the api documentation. See example below.
  3. Choose Create
  4. In the required field communication System, Choose the Communication System you created from Step 2.
  5. Please take note of the API-URL as you would need this to create SAP BTP Destination in a later step.
  6. User Name will be populated automatically because it have assigned it in the selected Communication system.
  7. Please take note of the Service URL/Service Interface information as you would need this to populate information of SM or UM connections in a later step. In addition, be aware that this part https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com changes depending on your S/4HANA Cloud system.
    e.g.

    Service

    Application Protocol

    Service URL/Service Instance

    G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X)

    Odata V2

    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM    

    Consolidation Task ‒ Read

    Odata V4

    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_consolidationtask/srvd_a2x/sap/consolidationtask/0001/

h. Scroll down to Outbound communication section. This is required only if you will do outbound scenarios however this blogpost is only limited to inbound scenario. In order to bypass the required User Name field, untick all Outbound Service Status.

i. Choose Save.

j. Perform steps a-i for the other required Communication Scenario if needed.

 

BTP Destination Configuration

Use below information to create Destination relevant to bridge S/4HANA Public Cloud to SAP PaPM Cloud.

  1. In SAP BTP Cockpit, go to the subaccount and Choose Destinations.
  2. Choose Create Destination and populate with below information

    Name

    Unique name of destination in current subaccount.
    e.g. S4toPaPMC

    Type

    Select “HTTP”.

    Description

    Any description for the destination name.

    URL

    API-URL from Step 3e of Create Communication Arrangement
    e.g. https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com

    Proxy Type

    Internet

    Authentication

    BasicAuthentication

    User

    User created from Step 1c of Create Communication User
    e.g. PAPMCS4_TECHUSER

    Password

    Proposed Password from Step 1c of Create Communication User

  3. Choose Save.

SAP PaPM Cloud SM Modeling – Read Data via Odata Connection

As you may have already know, SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model only supports Odata type V4 hence in this example, we will be using the information from Consolidation Task ‒ Read API.

  1. Login to Standard Model and go to Connection Management Application. By following the procedure on help portal documentation, Create an Odata Connection based from below data:

    Connection Name

    Unique name of connection in the tenant

    Connection Class

    Select “Default”

    Connection Source

    Select “Odata”

    Connection Description

    Any phrase you wish to describe details about the connection

    Destination Name

    Destination created from previous step e.g. S4toPaPMC

    Entity Set

    Service structure entity of the S/4HANA Cloud API e.g. ConsolidationTask

    Service Root

    Specifies the root of service URL from Step 3g of Communication Arrangement e.g.
    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_consolidationtask/srvd_a2x/sap/consolidationtask/0001/

    Maximum page size

    1000 (Default)




  2. In a Created Environment, configure a Model View Odata and feed the information of the connection created above. Activate the Model View Odata and choose ‘Run’ to populate the data.


SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling – Read Data via Odata Service Connection

OData V4

SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model supports both Odata V2 and Odata V4 and in terms of configuration they follow the same steps. Let’s take a look if we can load the data of both APIs mentioned above.

  1. Login to Universal Model and go to Connection Application. By following the procedure on help portal documentation, Create an Odata Service Connection based from data below:

    Environment

    Environment ID to where a connection will be created
    e.g. ODATAV4CON

    Connection Target Type

    Select “Odata Service”

    Connection Name

    Unique name of Connection within the Environment
    e.g. S/4HANACloud to Universal Model OData V4

    Description

    Any phrase you wish to describe details about the connection

    Model Filter

    Used to filter out the models coming from the target to the ones which match the expression (pattern). Supports JavaScript regular expressions. It is optional and can be empty.

    Destination Name

    Destination created from previous step e.g. S4toPaPMC

    Service Path

    Specifies the root of service URL from Step 3g of Communication Arrangement e.g.
    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_consolidationtask/srvd_a2x/sap/consolidationtask/0001/

    Default Key Field

    Existing environment field of data type “String” or “UUID” created by the user and not registered from a different connection.

    Parent Folder

    All models synced from the connection to the environment will have this folder function as parent. It is optional and can be empty.


  2. On Edit Mode, you can rename and remove the Models which you do not want to be added in the Environment
  3. Choose a Model e.g. ConsolidationTaskME. On the left hand side,
    a. Choose the checkbox
    b. Choose Add Fields button to register the field into the environment
    c. If you do not want to carry over the relation, you can uncheck this option.
    d. Apply Changes then Save on next dialog box.
  4. Choose Sync Model button to add the Model Entity to the environment.
  5. Setup Reader Function on top of the Model Entity. Make sure to add the fields in the reader which you want data to be populated. Activate the Environment and Run the Reader to populate data on Model Entity.

OData V2

Now that you are done with setting it up for ODataV4, How about for OdataV2?

Like I said previously, steps in setting up Odata V2 for SAP PaPM Cloud UM should be the same as Odata V4. The only difference here would be the Service Path information from S4HANA Cloud Service URL/Service Interface which classifies whether what application protocol is used in this case, I used below information:

Service Path

Specifies the root of service URL from Step 3g of Communication Arrangement e.g.
https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM

Follow steps 2-5 from Odata V4 Setup in order to bring the Model Entity created from Odata Service Connection and use a reader to buffer the data to it.

User1 and User 2 has completed their activity! Hoooraay!

This sums up one way of using S/4HANA Cloud Data into SAP PaPM Cloud. By mastering the steps outlined, you can be better equipped in your implementation project. With due diligence in checking, you can also try out other available APIs and create your own personal guide. Like I always said, this is one way and there can be another and a more optimized one.

I am not sure how to end this blogpost so sending you a goodluck gif instead 😅

entry.title: SAP CPI | Generate STFP Known Hosts File to CPI
entry.content:
Introduction : 

Today, I will discuss an important step we need to implement in our SFTP scenarios: creating a Host Key for SFTP and defining it in CPI. So why are we doing this? What happens if we don't perform this step?

SFTP Host Key is a cryptographic key used to authenticate the identity of an SFTP server. This key is stored and used by the client to ensure the server's reliability.

  1. Security: The Host Key is used to verify the identity of the server. When a connection is established, the client receives the server's public key and compares it to confirm the server's identity.

  2. Cryptography: Host Keys are typically generated using cryptographic algorithms such as RSA, DSA, or ECDSA. These keys ensure secure identification of the server.

  3. Connection Integrity: The client stores the Host Key in a "known hosts" file. This allows the server's identity to be verified in subsequent connections, ensuring secure communication.

  4. Protection Against Man-in-the-Middle Attacks: The Host Key enhances the security of communication between the client and server, protecting against unauthorized interference. If the server's key changes, the client receives a warning, indicating a potential security risk.

SFTP Host Key is a critical component for ensuring a secure and reliable connection.

After creating a host key for our SFTP server, we need to define it as a known host in CPI. This step is crucial for ensuring secure and reliable communication between the CPI and the SFTP server. By doing this, we enable the CPI to authenticate the server during each connection, preventing unauthorized access and enhancing overall security.

1. Log in to CPI with the S user.

2. Go to Monitoring → Connectivity Test screen.

3. In the Test Connectivity section, select the SSH tab and enter the SFTP host in the host field.

Authentication: None

After that, click on Send.

4. As a result, we click on the displayed Host Key and copy it into a .txt file. We save this file locally on the computer.

5. Go to Overview → Security Material.

We click on the Upload option and select Known Host (SSH), then choose the .txt file we saved locally to deploy it.

We can later check the accessibility of the service through Connectivity Test using the provided user and password. However, we first need to deploy the user and password as User Credentials in Security Materials.

Additionally;

Let’s assume that a file will be dropped to SFTP using CPI. After designing the scenario and deploying it for testing, if the known.host file is not uploaded, the service will encounter an error. An example error might look like this:

entry.title: SAP B1 - AP Invoice - Exchange Rate
entry.content:
Hi community,

On the AP Invoice screen, for SAP B1, please could anyone tell me how I could make the field for 'OPCH, DocRate' be visible but not active? We want it to show, but don't want users to be able to change the field value, the currency exchange rate is set for the year and it pulls through automatically. We have had a situation where a user has manually changed this field by mistake, and we're looking to stop this happening again! I've looked in the form settings but can't seem to see this field, it may be called something else in the form settings though?

Picture below, thanks in advance 🙂

entry.title: Re: No cost management is provided for sales document item- Message no. IX057
entry.content: Hello, I'm getting the same error message. To resolve it, you need to go to the contract, ‘Procurement’ tab, and the ‘requirement type’ must be ‘SE - Service item with cost management’. Regards Quentin ROOS

entry.title: Re: Creating IDoc Extensions
entry.content:
Hi DominikTylczyn,

I have a use case where I need to provide an additional segment with an not yet used qualifier - but only once per line item.

You can manipulate IDoc content and segments with EXIT_SAPLVEDF_002 (user-exit LVEDF001). The exit is called after each segment of INVOIC* is created. You should add extra segments to INT_EDIDD parameter of the EXIT_SAPLVEDF_002 function.

If the exit is called for each segment, how can we avoid adding the same segment+qualifier pair multiple times to the same line item?

CASE int_edidd-segnam.
  WHEN 'E1EDP02'.
    ls_e1edp02 = int_edidd-sdata.
      CASE ls_e1edp02-qualf.
        WHEN '044'. " This check would result in the segment never being added, as the segment does not exist.
          ... code to add segment with qualifier ...

Thank you in advance for any hints you can provide.

entry.title: EWM outbound delivery document flow not updated
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have seen this issue quite often, however not able to understand the main cause of it.
Document flow such as PCP (picking plan) or PCA (picking) is not updating in delivery document flow.

For instance, with respect to PCA link: The stock removal warehouse task is already confirmed, the stock has the corresponding reference, but the ODO item has only a PCP link, a corresponding PCA link is not available.


I am aware of how to fix this via EWM check monitor. However, to fix this permanently I am not able to reproduce this or not able to understand main cause for this. 
Any input is really useful, thank you.

entry.title: SQLITE_CONSTRAINT: UNIQUE constraint failed error during CAP Tutorial Execution
entry.content:
Hi CAP Experts, I am working on the CAP Tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-apm-nodejs-create-service.html#ad94d17b-8f0d-420e-b658-d44828bbee6b. In step 7 - add initial data, I added the data as per the tutorial though I observed that the "cds add data" command created ; separated fields. So, I changed my data like below. 

my.bookshop-Authors.csv

ID;name
101;Emily Brontë
107;Charlote Brontë
150;Edgar Allen Poe
170;Richard Carpenter

my.bookshop-Books.csv where I changed the column separators as , instead of ;

ID,title,author_ID,stock
201,Wuthering Heights,101,12
207,Jane Eyre,107,11
251,The Raven,150,333
252,Eleonora,150,555
271,Catweazle,170,22

But as soon as I save those files, I get below errors in cds watch

[Error: ENTITY_ALREADY_EXISTS] {
errno: 19,
code: 400,
originalMessage: 'SQLITE_CONSTRAINT: UNIQUE constraint failed: my_bookshop_Authors.ID',
numericSeverity: 4
}

Please help. SAP Cloud Application Programming Model 

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: Re: Unable to retrieve data from OData API for Reports fr...
entry.content:
Hello,

for the FINGLAU04 ( or in general for most of the Financial Reports) and CRMCIVIB ( Invoice Volume )

are you also providing mandatory selections?

For example : FINGLAU04 (or Report :FINGLAU04_Q0001), You will need to provide, Companu, Set of books, Accountign Period, Fiscal year, Reportign Structure, Balance sheet type but additionally, you also need to provide the currency conversion date (BYD_P_CURDATE)

 

 

Other option would be to fetch the data using : ana_businessanalytics_analytics.svc
URL should look something like  : https://<SystemURL>/sap/byd/odata/ana_businessanalytics_analytics.svc/RPFINGLAU04_Q0001QueryResults?

or Check using Postman and UI to see if the data exist and what selection fields are needed. 

Also Check : 
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/odata-for-sap-business-bydesign-analytics/ba-p/13142748

Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Addressing UNSDGs Through Women-Centric Education
entry.content:
As Global Goals Week kicks off at the United Nations Headquarters in New York, Barefoot College International (BCI) reflects on their pledge to address the Sustainability Development Goals (SDGs). Their vocational and livelihood programs holistically offer solutions to 15 of the 17 goals. In the spirit of “leaving no one behind,” BCI focuses their programmatic responses on the accessibility of education and resources for marginalized women.

Overall, it’s because of this women-centric approachthat 15 of the 17 goals are naturally addressed; women and girls of the Global South are often the last to receive educational and entrepreneurial resources. Thus, uplifting women accelerates global prosperity, peace, and Climate Justice, which are key components of the global goals.

SDG #1: No Poverty

Communities and societies are only as strong as their weakest link. Women, the most untapped resource in impoverished villages, have great potential when included in the workforce. Women are statistically responsible for more than three times as much domestic unpaid labor within the family unit. Offering women education, leading to better entrepreneurial and livelihood options locally, increases household incomes, resulting in a higher quality of life.

SDG #4: Quality Education

Offering women and girls educational resources not only provides them with chances at better-paid income jobs, but it also imparts valuable information on their health, including sexual health, planned parenthood, women’s rights, and nutrition. This increases living standards, improves sexual health, lowers the birth rate, and assists women with knowing their legal rights.

SDG #5: Gender Equality

Gender inequality stagnates social progress, due to the loss of potential workforce professionals when women are not included. BCI’s primary mission is to create lasting change from the ground up through harnessing the power of rural communities and their women. This includes ensuring that the next generation is motivated to keep girl children in school, creating a ripple effect of systemic change toward gender-balanced development.

SDG #10: Reduced Inequalities

When we tie poverty to gender inequality, focusing on the education of women is automatically a means to foster a more equitable playing field for rural communities. Often lagging behind their urban neighbors, remote regions have had an uphill battle in maintaining healthy literacy rates and digital savviness. This can be attributed to a lack of accessibility, thus offering these regions the tools to gain experience and knowledge helps them catch up and thrive.

SDG #17: Partnerships for the Goals

BCI has recognized the value of meaningful, lucrative partnerships with entities whose missions align with theirs. To achieve the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UNSDGs) in a timely manner globally, partnering with like-minded organizations is essential.

Call to action

To join forces with BCI, or to support their ongoing work focused on women, education, and sustainability, please visit their website, www.barefootcollege.org.

Also, be sure to check out the latest research from the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team and learn more about SAP’s “zero emissions, zero waste, and zero inequality” approach to sustainability.

entry.title: Re: Make your Legacy SAP WM a Stock Room Management Compliant for WCU Interfaces
entry.content:
Prakash,

I wish to understand this compatibility in the world of S4/Hana of moving from WM to SRM.  We are happy with the functionality of WM and we DO NOT use the 7 areas of Yard Management, Task Management etc..

The compatibility check comes up with a Red traffic light for Warehouse Ctrl Unit Interface and yellow for 2 others.  What should be our next step?  Given the December 25 deadline on WM, given that we are NOT using any of these areas, do we really need to do anything to turn these red/yellow to green?

pshrikanth@gmail.com is my id - your help will be highly valuable!

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HC Professional Services - 2nd Approval Level f...
entry.content:
Hi @Christophe88,

Currently in S/4HANA Public cloud there is only 1 approver for 1 timesheet entry.

Please use the Continous Influence Program ( https://www.sap.com/about/customer-involvement/influence-adopt.sap-continuous-influence.html ) to have the functionality enhanced to allow 2 approver level for a timesheet entry

For the latest updates on the planned function capabilities, please refer to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud roadmap. Available under https://www.sap.com/roadmaps, the roadmap is updated quarterly.

Regards,
Monisha.

entry.title: Re: Issues creating Hana DB Free tier instance on Azure
entry.content: Hi Alexander, (sorry for the formatting, have not figured out how to work the enter button on this forum)... Thanks for the suggestion. Yes, there is usage listed, but the sub account has been deleted and the copy on the items reads "This subaccount was deleted sometime within the last 12 months. It's still listed here because it has registered usage during this period. Its usage was last reported in September 2024. Because the subaccount no longer exists, we cannot display its metadata, such as its path or labels." I think that lost connection message I received on the first invocation somehow orphaned the instance... I was also hoping some weekend clean up job might rectify this... but when I created a new sub account (I only have had one at a time) I still get the message there can only be one! Lol.. What ever is running and associated to azure(west), I cannot get to... either in multi-env or cf. BTW, entitlements for the free tier are listed and assigned. what is weird is I can create and destroy the free tier hana in Azure (east)... Anyway, not sure what to do here aside from waiting the time period when SAP will destroy the instance due to inactivity.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HC Professional Services - 2nd Approval Level f...
entry.content: Hi Monisha, thank you for your quick reply. I will use de Influence Program as suggested. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: how to get all the dates enetered in the date range prompt in webi report even if not in DB tabl
entry.content: I don't have time to work out the details now, but you should be able to generated a list of your desired dates in a free-hand SQL statement with a CTE. Then merge that to your main query on the date.

entry.title: SAP PO version upgrade and security patch activity
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are currenty working on SAP PO 7.5 single stack SP28.. and we would like to upgrade to latest version available.. could you please let us know how do we find out the latest version of SAP PO.. also collecting the right versions for all components.. Also how do we check and conclude whether any security patch is needed for SAP PO environments? Could you please advise on this, Thank you!

Regards

Ramu.
